1.1a: Teacher explicitly announces objective at lesson start (e.g. 'اليوم سنتعلم…', 'هدف درسنا اليوم…'), Objective is repeated or referenced mid-lesson, Objective is written/displayed (inferred if teacher reads aloud from board)
1.1b: Teacher states what success looks like ('ستنجح إذا استطعت…', 'سأعرف أنك فهمت عندما…'), Teacher describes expected output/product standard, Reference to rubric, checklist, or exemplar
1.1c: Teacher connects task to objective ('هذا النشاط سيساعدنا في…'), Teacher references objective when transitioning between activities, Teacher closes lesson by returning to stated objective
1.2a: Concepts build from simple to complex (scaffold evident in transcript), Teacher activates prior knowledge before introducing new content, No unexplained conceptual jumps in the lesson flow
1.2b: Smooth verbal transitions between phases ('الآن ننتقل إلى…', 'انتهينا من… سنبدأ الآن…'), No extended dead-time periods (long silence or repeated off-topic talk), Teacher adjusts pace explicitly ('سنسرع قليلاً', 'لدينا وقت كافٍ')
1.2c: Teacher explicitly monitors time ('لدينا 5 دقائق فقط'), Teacher cuts or extends activity based on time, Lesson reaches intended closure vs. running out of time
2.1a: Teacher thinks aloud while solving/demonstrating ('أنا الآن أفكر في…', 'لاحظوا كيف أقوم…'), Teacher makes reasoning explicit, not just shows product, Teacher works through an example before students attempt
2.1b: Teacher and students work through an example together ('لنحل هذا معاً…'), Teacher prompts rather than tells during practice ('ما الخطوة التالية؟'), Teacher provides scaffolded support before releasing to independent work
2.2: Students verbally produce answers, summaries, explanations, Students write, draw, create, or manipulate materials, Teacher assigns tasks requiring student-generated output (not passive listening)
2.3: Teacher explicitly changes plan based on student confusion ('أرى أنكم لم تفهموا، سأشرح بطريقة أخرى'), Teacher re-explains, slows down, or simplifies after observing errors, Teacher extends or accelerates based on demonstrated mastery
3.1: Teacher gives explicit step-by-step instructions before tasks, Teacher checks instruction comprehension ('هل الجميع يعرف ماذا يفعل؟'), No repeated student questions about 'what to do' after instructions given
3.2a: Teacher uses affirming language ('ممتاز', 'أحسنتِ', 'فكرة جيدة'), Teacher uses students' names respectfully, No dismissive, sarcastic, or demeaning language toward students
3.2b: Teacher responds to wrong answers without shame ('شكراً، هذا يذكّرنا بـ… لكن…'), Teacher uses errors to re-teach or probe understanding ('لماذا تظنين هذا؟'), Teacher normalizes mistakes ('الخطأ طبيعي وهو جزء من التعلم')
4.1: Smooth opening routine without wasted time, Students respond to standard signals (hand raise, quiet signal) without explicit re-teaching, Materials distribution/collection is orderly and efficient
4.2: Teacher uses non-disruptive redirection ('اسمي X, أرجو الانتباه'), Teacher applies consistent consequences without escalation, Minimal disruption to instructional flow when managing behavior
5.1a: Teacher asks open-ended questions requiring reasoning ('لماذا؟', 'كيف توصلتِ؟', 'قارني بين…'), Questions require more than one-word/recall answers, Teacher probes with follow-up ('هل يمكنك التوسع؟', 'ماذا تقصدين بـ…؟')
5.1b: Teacher uses cold call (not only volunteers) ('من لم يُجب بعد…'), Teacher uses wait time before accepting answers, Teacher uses whole-class CFU ('أشيري إذا وافقت…', 'من تعتقد X ومن تعتقد Y؟'), Teacher conducts explicit comprehension check before moving on
5.2: Multiple distinct assessment methods used (oral Q&A + written + peer + self), Teacher uses more than one modality for checking understanding, Evidence of at least 2 different formative tools in the session
6.1: Teacher pauses to analyse a wrong answer aloud ('هذا الخطأ يدل على أن…'), Teacher notes a pattern of errors across students, Teacher uses student response as diagnostic evidence for next teaching move
6.2: Teacher re-explains using a different approach after detecting confusion, Teacher provides additional examples or scaffolding for struggling students, Teacher differentiates support within the lesson based on observed need
7.1: Teacher explicitly explains why a tool is being used ('سنستخدم هذا التطبيق لأنه يساعدنا في…'), Technology use directly enables student practice or understanding of the objective, Teacher references technology output in pedagogical discussion
7.2: Minimal off-task student behavior related to technology, Teacher manages technology transitions efficiently, No evidence of technology causing confusion or distraction
8.1: Teacher conducts explicit pre/post check within the same lesson, Students demonstrate better performance by end vs. start (audible in transcript), Teacher explicitly names progress ('في بداية الحصة لم تستطيعي… الآن…')
8.2: N/A — this KPI is always Insufficient from a single recording, Do not attempt to infer growth trends, grade improvement, or curriculum mastery from one lesson
